subject: Independent claim-by-claim reassessment of the verified context bootstrap resolver external audit v2

previous_position.statement: AUDIT-CLAUDE-VCBR-002 reports a real and growing rehydration cost, recommends schema hygiene before implementation, and characterizes the described resolver as mostly duplicative of the documented RAG federation contract.

claims_under_review.original_claim: Canonical rehydration has a material context cost of approximately 26,200 tokens.
claims_under_review.source_and_provenance: AUDIT-CLAUDE-VCBR-002.md plus canonical read order at parent b951aee9ec2f61dfa1bad2df2a18facb2c5471f8.
claims_under_review.method: Exact working-tree and Git blob byte counts; local tokenizer availability check; bytes/4 kept separate.
verified_observations: 105284 working-tree bytes
103273 Git blob bytes
26321 bytes/4 heuristic units
no local tokenizer available
favorable_evidence: The independently measured heuristic is close to the audit magnitude.
The route is large enough to create material context pressure.
contrary_evidence: No real token count was executed.
The full task-required corpus is larger than the minimal route and conditional loading can make actual cost smaller.
limitations: Line endings change working-tree versus Git blob bytes.
Token magnitude and model context impact are not established by bytes alone.
claims_under_review.consequences: Use exact bytes as the baseline and do not present 26321 as a real token count.

claims_under_review.original_claim: Append-only authorization and state history causes continuing documentary growth.
claims_under_review.baseline: Parent b951aee9 with 32 authorization records and 32 consumed transitions.
claims_under_review.method: Count records and state keys; classify transition and standing-flag lifecycles; measure repository history.
verified_observations: 32 authorization records are all consumed and retained.
32 consumed transitions remain in CURRENT_STATE.authorization_state.
The 14-file route grew across the measured history.
favorable_evidence: Every new consumed authorization adds a registry record and a state transition.
The canonical validator requires one-to-one state_key preservation.
contrary_evidence: The observed growth period is an intensive construction phase.
Future authorization frequency is unknown.
limitations: Append-only retention does not prove a future context-window failure date.
claims_under_review.consequences: Treat growth mechanism as real while avoiding unsupported linear break-date claims.

claims_under_review.original_claim: The authorization registry is a disproportionate component of canonical rehydration.
claims_under_review.method: Exact byte count and share calculation.
verified_observations: registry/authorizations.json is 32319 bytes.
It is 30.697 percent of the 105284-byte minimal route.
All 32 records are consumed.
favorable_evidence: It is the largest single measured source.
contrary_evidence: The registry is conditionally applicable under the canonical reading order rather than always necessary for every question.
Its retained history is required by current state_key integrity.
limitations: Byte share is not equivalent to latency or answer-quality impact.
claims_under_review.consequences: Any optimization must preserve state_key traceability and measure selection errors, not only byte reduction.

claims_under_review.original_claim: CURRENT_STATE.authorization_state conflates consumed transitions with standing permission flags.
claims_under_review.method: Resolve keys against registry state_key values and classify remaining entries by lifecycle and validator semantics.
verified_observations: 32 entries are consumed transitions resolved by state_key.
6 entries are fixed standing safety flags.
There are zero registry orphans and zero unregistered consumed transitions.
favorable_evidence: The two groups have different value types and lifecycle semantics.
contrary_evidence: The current schema intentionally permits an unconstrained object.
The validator explicitly recognizes this mixed representation and it currently passes.
limitations: Calling the representation a schema defect is normative; factual conflation is independently observable.
claims_under_review.consequences: A future separation would require coordinated schema, validator, fixture and continuity review and is not authorized here.

claims_under_review.original_claim: The proposed BOOTSTRAP_CONTEXT.md selected-content artifact is non-conforming with the ChatGPT continuity rule.
claims_under_review.source_and_provenance: Audit v2 description of proposal section 8.10 contrasted with docs/CONTINUITY_PROTOCOL.md.
claims_under_review.baseline: Canonical rule that any ChatGPT bootstrap attachment may declare only repository, branch and entrypoint.
claims_under_review.method: Compare the behavior explicitly attributed by audit v2 with the canonical rule.
verified_observations: The pointer-only bootstrap rule is explicit.
Audit v2 describes section 8.10 as a selected-content bootstrap artifact.
No exact proposal source was available.
favorable_evidence: Selected canonical content in a bootstrap attachment would violate the pointer-only rule.
contrary_evidence: The exact proposal text and artifact contract could not be inspected.
An ephemeral technical reference manifest outside the bootstrap attachment role is not clearly adjudicated by the rule.
limitations: Specific proposal non-conformance cannot be fully reproduced without the proposal.
claims_under_review.consequences: Do not generate BOOTSTRAP_CONTEXT.md and classify alternative-manifest compatibility as insufficient until a complete source is available.

claims_under_review.original_claim: The described resolver substantially duplicates RAG-FEDERATION-CONTRACT-001.
claims_under_review.method: Compare audit-described resolver components with scope, namespace, commit, authority, conflict, citation and cache stages.
verified_observations: The audit-described components map to all eight RAG stages.
The RAG contract already contains controls absent from the audit-described proposal.
The exact resolver proposal was unavailable.
favorable_evidence: Contradiction detection and cited context map directly to stages 7 and 8.
contrary_evidence: Token budget planning is not present in the RAG contract.
A resolver utility could implement rather than duplicate the governing contract.
Exact component boundaries are not independently inspectable.
limitations: Substantial overlap is verified; complete isomorphism is not.
claims_under_review.consequences: Do not establish a parallel governing architecture; require any future implementation proposal to declare which RAG stages it implements.

claims_under_review.original_claim: Resolver invalidation on any remote HEAD change is too coarse and duplicates a better canonical namespace-scoped rule.
claims_under_review.source_and_provenance: Audit v2 description and RAG index_contract.
claims_under_review.method: Verify canonical invalidation controls and compare them with behavior attributed to the resolver.
verified_observations: Affected-namespace-only rebuild is canonical.
Stale removal and exact commit pinning are canonical.
The exact resolver invalidation algorithm was unavailable.
favorable_evidence: A global invalidation would be strictly coarser than the documented contract.
contrary_evidence: Audit v2 is the only available source for the resolver behavior.
No implementation measurements exist for invalidation cost or correctness.
limitations: The canonical alternative is verified; the challenged algorithm is not independently reproduced.
claims_under_review.consequences: Keep namespace-scoped invalidation as the governing requirement for any future experiment.

claims_under_review.original_claim: Authority, project, state and active-commit resolution must be deterministic before semantic retrieval.
claims_under_review.baseline: Current canonical startup and retrieval rules.
claims_under_review.method: Inspect mandatory read order and RAG retrieval_pipeline ordering.
verified_observations: authority_precedes_similarity is true.
Stages 1 through 4 resolve scope, namespaces, commits and authority/status before retrieval.
Only stages 5 and 6 require semantic behavior.
favorable_evidence: The rule preserves canonical authority and project isolation.
contrary_evidence: Determinism alone does not prove selection completeness or lower total context cost.
limitations: The contract is documented but not implemented.
claims_under_review.consequences: Any future experiment must treat deterministic authority gates as invariants.

claims_under_review.original_claim: A token-reduction threshold is sufficient to select an implementation alternative.
claims_under_review.source_and_provenance: Audit v2 threshold discussion and authorization 063 comparison requirements.
claims_under_review.baseline: No locally executed token count and no discriminating fixture experiment.
claims_under_review.method: Check tokenizer availability and availability of precision, false-positive, false-negative and omitted-evidence measurements.
verified_observations: No real token count was executed.
Selection precision, false positives and false negatives were not measured.
No alternative was implemented.
favorable_evidence: Absolute and percentage reduction are useful metrics.
contrary_evidence: A percentage alone cannot establish authority preservation, completeness, maintenance or failure safety.
limitations: No statistical sample or acceptance threshold calibration exists.
claims_under_review.consequences: Do not approve an implementation from token reduction alone.

claims_under_review.original_claim: Schema hygiene can safely remove roughly one third of the common rehydration cost without new components.
claims_under_review.source_and_provenance: Audit v2 alternative F-prime plus independent byte and consumer inventory.
claims_under_review.method: Project omission of the authorization registry when no active authorizations exist and inventory affected consumers.
verified_observations: Theoretical omission is 32319 working-tree bytes or 30.697 percent.
Residual route would be 72965 working-tree bytes.
Current schemas, validator, fixture and continuity semantics would require coordinated change.
favorable_evidence: The projected byte reduction is material.
The registry can remain canonical rather than be archived.
contrary_evidence: No active_authorizations field exists.
No consumer behavior or omission safety was tested.
Schema change and migration are explicitly unauthorized.
limitations: Projected byte savings are not measured token savings.
Selection precision and omitted contrary evidence remain unknown.
claims_under_review.consequences: Retain as a candidate for a separately authorized experiment, not an approved implementation.

claims_under_review.original_claim: schemas/brief.schema.json already implements token budget planning.
claims_under_review.source_and_provenance: Audit v2 self-correction contrasted directly with schemas/brief.schema.json.
claims_under_review.method: Inspect required fields and all property definitions.
verified_observations: The schema has no token budget field.
The schema has no context limit, priority order or omission log.
scope is an unrestricted object.
contrary_evidence: All inspected schema content contradicts the duplication claim.
claims_under_review.consequences: Preserve the audit v2 correction; token budget planning is not duplicated by the brief schema.

claims_under_review.original_claim: VERIFIED_CONTEXT_BOOTSTRAP_RESOLVER should be treated as a new governing architecture.
claims_under_review.source_and_provenance: Audit-described resolver and canonical RAG federation contract.
claims_under_review.method: Separate governing contract from possible implementation utilities.
verified_observations: Most described responsibilities already have a canonical RAG stage or control.
A future resolver would create new implementation surface.
The complete proposal is unavailable.
favorable_evidence: A resolver package, CLI or manifest vocabulary would be a new component if built.
contrary_evidence: Its described governing semantics are largely pre-existing.
Creating a parallel authority model would conflict with canonical ownership.
limitations: Exact non-overlapping responsibilities cannot be enumerated without the proposal.
claims_under_review.consequences: Do not approve it as an independent architecture.

claims_under_review.original_claim: The described deterministic resolver is better classified as a partial implementation of RAG-FEDERATION-CONTRACT-001 with semantic stages disabled.
claims_under_review.source_and_provenance: Audit v2 mapping and canonical eight-stage RAG pipeline.
claims_under_review.method: Map explicit audit-described resolver responsibilities to the canonical pipeline and controls.
verified_observations: Six deterministic stages cover scope, namespaces, commits, authority/status, conflicts and cited context.
Semantic retrieval and ranking are isolated to stages 5 and 6.
Token budget planning remains outside the RAG contract.
favorable_evidence: The functional mapping is strong and preserves a single authority model.
contrary_evidence: The exact proposal may contain responsibilities not represented in the audit summary.
No implementation exists to establish actual contract conformance.
limitations: This is an architectural classification, not implementation approval.
claims_under_review.consequences: If implementation is ever considered, require an explicit conformance matrix rather than a new parallel contract.

evidence_supporting_change.finding: Exact bytes, authorization correspondence, consumer inventory and historical growth were independently reproduced.
The documented contract contains deterministic authority gates, semantic stages, namespace invalidation, citations, conflicts, cache role and safe failure states.
Bootstrap attachments are pointer-only and consumed transitions must resolve by state_key.
The schema contains no token budgeting semantics.

evidence_against_change.finding: The mixed authorization_state representation is currently validated and internally consistent.
No real token count, resolver fixture experiment or exact proposal source was available.
RAG contracts are documented but not implemented; overlap does not establish working equivalence.

reasoning_summary: The external audit correctly identifies material byte cost, append-only authorization growth, type conflation, a pointer-only bootstrap rule, brief-schema self-correction and substantial overlap with the RAG federation contract. Its exact token count, resolver equivalence, invalidation behavior and implementation-selection thresholds cannot be fully confirmed because no tokenizer, complete resolver proposal or discriminating experiment was available. Schema hygiene remains a candidate, not an approved or demonstrated solution.
consequences: Register audit v2 as external evidence with no authority effect.
Record only the supersession relation declared by v2 because v1 is unavailable.
Preserve exact bytes and label bytes/4 as a heuristic.
Do not implement resolver, RAG, CLI, schemas, runtime or product changes.
Preserve RAG-FEDERATION-CONTRACT-001 as the single documented retrieval-governance architecture.
Require a separate authorization before any experiment or implementation consequence.

recommended_action.purpose: Compare no-change, schema-hygiene simulation, deterministic reference selection and contract-conformant deterministic RAG stages using pinned synthetic fixtures.
required_metrics: real tokenizer counts if locally available without installation
